objects.name: Banking Organization (TTX participant)
objects.description: Retail bank operating a national ATM fleet.

objects.name: ATM switch and cash access services
objects.description: Infrastructure supporting ATM transaction routing, authorization, and availability monitoring.

objects.name: Disrupt ATM transaction routing and cash access
objects.description: Adversary targets ATM switching services to cause broad customer-facing availability issues.

objects.name: ATM network disruption during holiday weekend
objects.description: Simulated outage affecting ATM withdrawals, balances, and branch customer overflow.

objects.content: Customers across multiple regions report failed ATM withdrawals and duplicate debit holds, while branch call volumes surge.

objects.abstract: Inject 2: public trust and liquidity management
objects.content: The bank must decide how to ration emergency cash support, adjust fee refunds, publish status updates, and coordinate with the processor while rumors spread online.

objects.name: Emergency customer cash access plan
objects.description: Activate branch and partner-channel fallback measures to preserve customer access to cash.

objects.name: Transparent public status communications
objects.description: Provide frequent customer updates, fee-remediation guidance, and branch support instructions.

objects.abstract: Inject 3: cyber suspicion intensifies and service restoration is delayed
objects.content: Cyber operations identify anomalies consistent with targeted disruption of transaction routing logic or supporting service dependencies. There is still no evidence of customer account compromise, but restoration remains uncertain. Branches report rising confrontational customer interactions, and the contact center is exceeding service-level targets.

objects.abstract: Inject 4: service restoration, backlog management, and post-incident obligations
objects.content: The processor confirms stability has been restored, but the bank's cyber and technology teams require validation checks before full reconnection and normalization. Transaction backlogs, customer complaints, fee reversals, and branch cash replenishment issues remain active. Senior leadership requests a preliminary impact summary and regulator-ready timeline.

objects.name: TTX Scenario 4 - ATM switch outage and cash access disruption
objects.description: Scenario tests customer-impact management, operational coordination, and public trust response.
